name: Los Fayos and Grisel Loop via Mirador de la Diezma
name_original: Cueva del Castillo - Mirador de la Diezma - Grisel — circular desde los Fayos
description: Hike a moderate 10.2-mile (16.4 km) circular route from Los Fayos, featuring panoramic views from Mirador de la Diezma and historic castle r
region_intro: Hiking around Los Fayos offers diverse terrain, characterized by dramatic conglomerate cliffs and the foothills of Moncayo Natural Park. The region features the scenic Val reservoir and the Queiles River, providing varied landscapes for outdoor activities. Oak forests and unique geological formations contribute to the area's distinct character. This setting provides a range of hiking experiences, from riverside paths to more elevated trails. Best hiking trails around Los Fayos The most popular hiking route is Castilian-Aragonese Jacobean Way: Tarazona-Ágreda, a 9.0 miles (14.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 54 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a steady elevation gain through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Los Fayos and Grisel Loop via Mirador de la Diezma, a moderate 10.2 miles (16.4 km) path. This trail offers views of the surrounding cliffs and valleys. Local hikers also love the La Marcuela and Hayedo del Moncayo Loop from Vozmediano, a 9.4 miles (15.2 km) trail leading through Moncayo's oak forests, often completed in about 4 hours 37 minutes. Hiking around Los Fayos is defined by conglomerate cliffs, the Moncayo Natural Park, and the Queiles River. The network of 79 routes offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Los Fayos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 150 reviews. More than 1400 hikers have used komoot to explore Los Fayos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Los Fayos?
A: Los Fayos offers a wide network of 79 hiking routes. These include 19 easy trails, 50 moderate trails, and 10 more challenging options, catering to various fitness levels and preferences.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Los Fayos?
A: Yes, Los Fayos has 19 easy trails perfect for beginners or families. These routes often follow the scenic Queiles River or explore the lower areas around the Val reservoir, providing gentle terrain and beautiful views without significant elevation gain. The region's Aromatic Park also offers a pleasant, accessible experience.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Los Fayos?
A: Hikers in Los Fayos will encounter dramatic conglomerate cliffs, numerous caves, and the foothills of the Moncayo Natural Park. The area is also characterized by the scenic Val reservoir and the Queiles River, along with beautiful oak forests. For those interested in flora and fauna, the region is a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA) and features an Aromatic Park.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Los Fayos?
A: Yes, Los Fayos offers several excellent circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979985516/los-fayos-and-grisel-loop-via-mirador-de-la-diezma" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Fayos and Grisel Loop via Mirador de la Diezma&lt;/a&gt;, which provides expansive views of the surrounding cliffs and valleys. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059070187/la-marcuela-and-hayedo-del-moncayo-loop-from-vozmediano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Marcuela and Hayedo del Moncayo Loop from Vozmediano&lt;/a&gt;, leading through the beautiful oak forests of Moncayo.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to visit near the hiking trails?
A: While hiking, you can explore unique sites like the &lt;em&gt;Ruta de las Cuevas&lt;/em&gt;, which includes the legendary Cueva de Caco. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit&lt;/a&gt; offers splendid panoramic views, and the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Veruela&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby. Don't miss the Ermita de San Benito, a hermitage excavated directly into the rock.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Los Fayos?
A: The trails in Los Fayos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from dramatic cliffs to serene river paths, and the unique geological formations. The variety of routes, catering to different skill levels, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Can I hike to the source of the Queiles River?
A: Yes, you can hike to the source of the Queiles River. A notable route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550764" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Queiles River – Aldehuela de Ágreda loop from Vozmediano&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a chance to explore this significant natural feature and the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there opportunities for birdwatching while hiking in Los Fayos?
A: Absolutely. The "Sierra del Moncayo-Los Fayos-Sierra de Armas" area is designated as a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA), making it an excellent spot for ornithology. Hikers can observe a variety of species, including rupicolous, aquatic, and steppe birds, as well as insectivores.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Los Fayos?
A: The Moncayo region, including Los Fayos, offers pleasant hiking conditions during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Spring brings blooming flora, while autumn showcases vibrant fall colors in the oak forests. Summer can be warm, but higher elevation trails in Moncayo Natural Park provide cooler options. Winter hiking is possible, especially on lower trails, but requires appropriate gear for potentially colder conditions.

Q: Are there any long-distance or historical routes passing through Los Fayos?
A: Yes, the historic Camino Antonino, also known as the Camino del Agua, passes through Los Fayos. This route connects Tudela to Soria, offering a scenic and culturally rich walk along the river. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e972133266/castilian-aragonese-jacobean-way-tarazona-agreda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castilian-Aragonese Jacobean Way: Tarazona-Ágreda&lt;/a&gt; is a 9-mile trail that traverses varied landscapes in the region.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Moncayo Summit?
A: Many trails in the Los Fayos area provide views of the imposing Pico Moncayo, especially those leading into the Moncayo Natural Park. For direct access and panoramic views from the summit itself, consider routes that ascend to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit&lt;/a&gt;.
tips:
The Cueva del Castillo in Los Fayos is a fascinating place with a rich history. Located in the municipality of Los Fayos, in the province of Zaragoza, Aragon, this natural cave has been inhabited since the Bronze Age. In the 12th century, a defensive castle was built taking advantage of the natural cavity in the rock.
The castle of Los Fayos played a strategic role on the borders between the kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre and Castile. Although today only a few vestiges remain, such as the cistern and the water collection system, the cave remains a place of historical and archaeological interest.

The entrance to the cave is located in a vertical cut in the rock, and a ladder is needed to access it. Inside the cave, several rooms can be seen, including the hearth and the cistern.

Magnificent views of the Val dam
and the town of Los Fayos from the viewpoint of the castle. Of course, to get here we must overcome the 333 steps there are, but without a doubt, the reward is spectacular.

We are at the foot of the imposing windmills near the town of Grisel. We will have spectacular views of Moncayo and the Tarazona plain and other mountains that we can see on the information/geographic panel.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Cueva del Castillo - Mirador de la Diezma - Grisel circular route?
faqs.answer: This circular route offers a rich blend of natural beauty and historical sites. Key highlights include the stunning panoramic views from the Mirador de La Diezma Viewpoint, the historical Cueva del Castillo de Los Fayos with its ancient cave and castle remains, and the unique circular dry stone huts known as 'casillas de pico' found around Grisel. You'll also pass the Viewpoint of the Castle Towers of Los Fayos.

faqs.question: How challenging is this hiking route?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as moderate. While some sections are straightforward, it includes ascents to viewpoints like the Mirador de la Diezma and features 'cuestas muy empinadas' (very steep slopes). Reaching the viewpoint at the Castillo de Los Fayos involves climbing 333 steps, which can be quite demanding.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter varied terrain, moving through forested areas, agricultural lands with vineyards and olive groves, and more exposed elevated sections. The geological features are notable, including dry stone constructions and the 'Pozo de los Aines,' a natural sinkhole with lush vegetation.

faqs.question: Is the Cueva del Castillo - Mirador de la Diezma - Grisel route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in agricultural areas, near livestock, and in any historical or protected sites. Given the varied terrain and potential for steep sections, ensure your dog is fit for a moderate hike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Moncayo region can experience hot summers and cold winters. Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking, offering milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. During these times, you'll avoid the intense summer heat and potential snow or ice in winter, especially at higher elevations like the Mirador de la Diezma.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this circular hiking route or its natural features. However, always respect private property and local regulations.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular hike?
faqs.answer: The route is described as 'circular desde los Fayos,' suggesting Los Fayos as a primary starting point. You would typically look for parking within or on the outskirts of Los Fayos to begin your hike. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but village centers often have public parking options.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Camino Jacobeo Castellano-Aragonés: Tarazona-Ágreda, the Camino Natural del Agua Soriano. Camino Antonino, and Etapa 1: Tarazona - Santuario del Moncayo. It also briefly touches the [CIMA ZA05] Moncayo * Tarazona and the Camino de la Vera Cruz (El camino de los Templarios).

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from the Mirador de la Diezma?
faqs.answer: From the Mirador de La Diezma Viewpoint, which sits at 799 meters, you'll get panoramic views of the Moncayo mountain range, the Queiles river valley, the Ebro Depression, and surrounding towns like Tarazona, Grisel, and Tudela. On clear days, you might even see the Pyrenees and the Sierra de Guara. Informational panels at the viewpoint help identify landmarks.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation in Grisel or Los Fayos?
faqs.answer: Both Grisel and Los Fayos are small villages. While specific amenities aren't detailed, you can generally expect basic services. It's advisable to check local listings for cafes, pubs, or accommodation options in advance, especially if you plan to stay overnight or rely on them for refreshments.

faqs.question: What makes the 'casillas de pico' unique along this route?
faqs.answer: The 'casillas de pico' are unique circular dry stone huts found around Grisel and Monte de la Diezma. These structures are built without mortar, showcasing traditional agricultural architecture. They served as shelters for farmers and shepherds for over a century, offering a glimpse into the region's historical rural life.
